DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-07-2024 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. ISAAC ABGINESAZ, Esq. of Wilshire Law Firm (tel: 213-381-9988) for Plaintiffs Armond Farve, Angela Farve and Candace Farve.
Atty. JAMES HOLDER, Esq. of Gordon & Rees, Scully, Mansukhani LLP (tel: 415-986-5900) for Defendants Gaetani Real Estate and Loron Investments LLC

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT GAETANI REAL ESTATE AND LORON INVESTMENTS LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Gaetani Real Estate and Loron Investments, LLC's demurrer to the first amended complaint is sustained with prejudice; the suit is time-barred as to Gaetani and Loron by CCP 335.1.
Plaintiffs' 73-year-old decedent, with "a history of dementia and drug use," allegedly died of exposure in December 2020 in a parking garage owned by Loron and managed by Gaetani. (RJN Ex. 1 2:2-14.) On October 29, 2021, a wrongful death suit named Loron and Gaetani (among other defendants) and pled detailed allegations against them. That suit was dismissed without prejudice on April 25, 2022.
New counsel filed a second wrongful death suit on December 7, 2022 regarding the same events. This time, Loron and Gaetani were not named until plaintiffs designated them Doe defendants on April 4 and 6, 2023. That was months after CCP 335.1's two-year wrongful death limitations period had expired, and a plaintiff may not use the Doe convention unless "ignorant of the name of a defendant." (CCP 474; Woo v. Sup. Ct. (1999) 75 Cal.App.4th 169, 177 ["if the identity ignorance requirement of section 474 is not met, a new defendant may not be added after the statute of limitations has expired"].)
Plaintiffs oppose the demurrer with three arguments, all unavailing.
First, it is claimed that pleading the words "on or about" December 14, 2020 is "sufficient to encompass" plaintiffs' Doe "filings on April 4, 2023 and April 6, 2023" and thus avoid the statute of limitations. (Opp. 1:28-2:4.) It is not. Plaintiffs' first suit pled that decedent "became trapped in the parking garage during the weekend of December 12, 2020 and ultimate[sic] perished" in the bitter cold. (RJN Ex. 1 1:14.) Hence, "on or about December 14, 2020" logically means death some time on that weekend with discovery of the body on Monday, December 14, 2020. Most importantly, no one contends decedent died of cold exposure in springtime 2023, years later.
Second, current plaintiffs' counsel says she "was not apprised of the earlier lawsuit" until defense counsel informed her of it. (Perry Dec. 7:7-8; Opp. 2:5-6.) This argument is supported by that mere sentence in counsel's declaration. No description of client-intake procedures. No declaration from any client. No legal authority. No explanation of how the first complaint - available in seconds on this court's public website - could have been missed.
Third, counsel says plaintiffs "may not have sufficiently understood that their prior counsel filed the first lawsuit." (Opp. 2:7-8; Perry Dec. 7:9-11.) Again, this is supported by only a sentence in counsel's declaration. No client declaration, much less one that claims a prior lawyer drafted, filed and served a detailed complaint without any client's knowledge.
The request for judicial notice is granted.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint; Declaration Of Ariella E. Perry, Esq.
Off Calendar Nov-02-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF ARMOND FARVE'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

Appearances via Zoom: Ariella Perry of the Wilshire Law Firm (213.381.9988) for plaintiff and moving party; James Holder of Gordon Rees (415.986.5900) for defendant Gaetani Real Estate and Loron Investments, LLC; No other appearances.

Argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion for leave to file a first amended complaint is granted.

"That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) A proposed amended complaint's validity is not considered when deciding leave to amend. (See Kittredge Sports Co. v. Sup. Ct. (1989) 213 Cal.App.3d 1045, 1048.)

The complaint was filed less than a year ago and the current trial date is not until March 2024.

Mr. Holder requests to continue the trial date. The Court informs counsel that trial continuance requests are to be presented to the Presiding Judge in department 206. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/RBU)
